title: 2026 Honda City Facelift: New Tech and Refined Style for India’s Favorite Sedan
description: Honda is ready to update the legendary City sedan with its second major facelift, bringing a 360-degree camera, refreshed aesthetics, and improved hybrid tech.
content: Honda Cars India has confirmed the launch of the 2026 City Facelift on May 22, aimed at keeping the sedan competitive against the Skoda Slavia and Hyundai Verna.
The update features a redesigned front grille, sharper LED headlight graphics, and new multi-spoke alloy wheels to enhance its premium road presence.
A major interior addition is the 360-degree camera system, filling a key gap in its feature list, alongside new upholstery and updated trim inserts.
The powertrain remains unchanged: the reliable 121 PS 1.5L i-VTEC petrol and the ultra-efficient 126 PS e:HEV hybrid engine.
Safety is a priority, with the Honda Sensing ADAS suite now expected to be even more refined for Indian road conditions.
pros: New 360-degree camera for easier parking
Class-leading fuel efficiency in the Hybrid variant
Timeless design with fresh 2026 updates
Comprehensive Honda Sensing safety suite
cons: No turbocharged engine option for performance enthusiasts
Ground clearance remains a concern on large speed breakers
Infotainment screen size is smaller than some rivals

comparison.advantage: The City continues to lead in rear-seat comfort and hybrid efficiency, whereas the Verna focuses more on turbo-performance.

title: MG Majestor: Pre-Bookings Now Available for MG’s Premier SUV
description: The anticipation is almost over as MG Motor India officially kicks off pre-bookings for the Majestor, a luxury ladder-frame SUV directly competing with the Toyota Fortuner.
content: MG Motor India has launched pre-bookings for the MG Majestor with a nominal fee of ₹41,000, and the price is expected to be disclosed on May 10, 2026.
Positioned above the Gloster, the Majestor impresses with its striking matrix-style grille and robust 19-inch dual-tone alloy wheels.
Beneath the hood, this powerhouse is equipped with a 2.0L twin-turbo diesel engine, generating 215 PS and 478 Nm of torque, mated to a refined ZF 8-speed automatic transmission.
Inside, it raises the bar for the segment with 12-way power-adjustable front seats featuring ventilation and various massage settings.
For off-road aficionados, the 'M-Hub' all-terrain system provides 10 distinct drive modes, three differential locks, and a water-wading capacity of 810mm.
pros: Exceptional luxury features (massage seats, JBL audio)
True off-road capability with triple differential locks
Generous 6 and 7-seater configurations available
Level 2 ADAS for improved safety on highways
cons: High ground clearance makes entering and exiting a challenge
No petrol engine option available for NCR customers
Brand image still evolving compared to the iconic Fortuner

comparison.advantage: The Majestor distinguishes itself with a more technologically advanced cabin and enhanced ride comfort in contrast to the Fortuner's rugged and basic interior.

title: Tata Curvv EV: The Most Awaited Coupe SUV Launch of 2026
description: Tata Motors is poised to shake up the electric vehicle market with its innovative Curvv EV, showcasing coupe aesthetics, an impressive range, and cutting-edge technology.
content: Tata Motors is preparing to unveil its most fashionable electric SUV to date, the Curvv EV, in India in 2026.
The Curvv is distinctive with its coupe SUV silhouette, characterized by a sleek roofline, wide LED light strips, and a strikingly modern design.
Built on Tata’s Gen 2 EV platform, it is anticipated to achieve a driving range exceeding 500 km on a single charge.
The interior will boast a sleek dashboard, a sizable touchscreen infotainment system, and integrated connected car capabilities.
The Curvv EV is expected to feature fast-charging capabilities along with advanced safety features like ADAS.
pros: Stylish coupe SUV design
Extensive driving range
State-of-the-art EV platform from Tata
Sophisticated connected technology and ADAS
cons: Charging infrastructure is currently developing
Anticipated higher pricing
New platform’s reliability is still to be validated

title: MG Majestor: The New Flagship SUV King Arrives on April
description: MG is unveiling its most luxurious SUV yet! The Majestor is ready to hit the market next week, aiming to take on the Fortuner with its 215hp twin-turbo diesel engine and exceptional luxury.
content: The anticipation for MG's latest flagship model is nearly at an end. On April 20, 2026, the MG Majestor will officially take the position of MG's premium ICE vehicle in India, superseding the Gloster.

This isn't merely a refreshed design; it's a complete overhaul. The Majestor boasts a striking 'Matrix-style' front grille, 19-inch alloy wheels, and a revamped rear featuring connected LED tail-lights that make a bold statement on the road.

Under the hood, this SUV is equipped with a robust 2.0-litre twin-turbo diesel engine, delivering 215hp and 478Nm of torque. It comes with a ZF 8-speed automatic transmission and an impressive 4x4 system known as 'M-Hub,' which offers 10 selectable terrain modes.

Inside the cabin, you’ll discover dual 12.3-inch displays, a top-notch 12-speaker JBL sound system, and seats designed for massage therapy. Safety features are outstanding with Level 2 ADAS, plus a remarkable 810mm water-wading capability for those who venture off the beaten path.
With pre-bookings starting at ₹41,000, the Majestor is predicted to retail around ₹40 Lakh, positioning it as a highly appealing, tech-laden rival to the Toyota Fortuner Legender.
pros: Advanced technology and features surpassing those of the Fortuner
Genuine 4x4 components with triple differential locks
Impressive water-wading capacity (810mm)
Exclusive 5-year warranty package for the first 3,000 bookings
cons: Larger size may pose challenges in navigating through congested city traffic
Resale value generally lags behind competitors from Toyota

title: Tata Harrier EV AWD: The Ultimate Electric SUV Arrives Next Month
description: Tata Motors is set to debut the Harrier EV this May. Boasting dual-motor AWD, over 500 km of range, and cutting-edge V2L technology, this is the revolutionary SUV we've all been anticipating.
content: The electric SUV scene is on the brink of a major transformation. Tata Motors has officially announced that the eagerly awaited Harrier EV will arrive in Indian dealerships by late May 2026.

Built on Tata’s advanced Acti.ev platform, the Harrier EV keeps the bold and muscular presence of its diesel version, while introducing modern elements such as a closed front grille, streamlined alloy wheels, and full-width LED light strips at both ends.

A key highlight is the launch of a dual-motor All-Wheel Drive (AWD) system, marking it as Tata's first electric vehicle with authentic off-road capabilities. The sizable 60+ kWh battery is projected to provide a real-world range comfortably exceeding 450 km.

Inside, features reach new heights with a state-of-the-art 12.3-inch infotainment display, the Arcade.ev app suite, a panoramic sunroof, and Vehicle-to-Load (V2L) technology, enabling you to power external devices directly from the vehicle.
With a starting price estimated around ₹24 Lakh, the Harrier EV is poised to significantly challenge luxury electric SUVs while competing against models like the Mahindra XUV.e8 and BYD Atto 3.

specs.engine: Dual Motor AWD / Single Motor FWD

pros: True AWD capability makes it an adventurous electric SUV
Spacious interior and commanding road presence
V2L (Vehicle-to-Load) and V2V (Vehicle-to-Vehicle) charging functionalities
Enhanced Level 2 ADAS suite standard on higher trims
cons: The hefty battery pack will notably increase the overall weight
The anticipated high price for top-spec variants could rival entry-level luxury EVs

title: Honda Elevate e:HEV Hybrid: The Ultimate SUV Rival Arrives This May
description: Honda is set to introduce its acclaimed hybrid technology to the Elevate SUV, promising impressive mileage, competitive pricing, and innovative tech features.
content: After much anticipation, Honda Cars India has confirmed that the Elevate e:HEV (Strong Hybrid) will officially launch in late May 2026.
This hybrid variant will stand out visually, equipped with blue-accented Honda badges, a revamped front grille, and unique 17-inch aerodynamic alloy wheels.

The standout feature lies under the hood. It utilizes the reliable 1.5-litre Atkinson cycle strong hybrid powertrain adapted from the City e:HEV, effortlessly transitioning between electric-only, hybrid, and traditional engine drive modes.

This configuration promises industry-leading fuel efficiency, boasting an anticipated real-world mileage exceeding 25 kmpl, making it a direct competitor to the Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara and Toyota Hyryder hybrids.
The interior boasts a notable technology upgrade, featuring a fully digital instrument cluster, an electronic parking brake with auto-hold functionality, and an enhanced Honda Sensing (ADAS) suite.
pros: Outstanding fuel efficiency for a mid-size SUV
Smooth and sophisticated hybrid driving experience
Roomy and functional cabin with excellent visibility
Honda Sensing ADAS suite ensures superior safety
cons: Slight reduction in boot space due to battery pack placement
Higher expected entry price compared to standard petrol versions

title: Maruti Suzuki eVitara: The Game-Changing Electric SUV Arriving Next Month
description: India's leading car manufacturer is stepping into the electric vehicle market! Here’s everything you need to know about the Maruti Suzuki eVitara, its battery options, range, and anticipated pricing.
content: The anticipation is nearly over for Maruti Suzuki's inaugural all-electric offering. The production version of the eVX concept, now called the eVitara, is slated to arrive in Indian dealerships come May 2026.
Developed on a specialized EV skateboard platform in partnership with Toyota, the eVitara features an extended wheelbase that positions the wheels at the extremities, optimizing cabin space.
Buyers can choose between two battery packs—a 48 kWh and a more substantial 60 kWh version—promising a real-world range of up to 500 km on a single charge.
Inside, the eVitara is set to bring a significant technological upgrade for Maruti, showcasing a dual-screen floating display, a rotary drive selector, and a sleek, organized dashboard layout.
With a projected starting price of ₹15 Lakh, it aims to pose a strong challenge to rivals such as the Tata Curvv EV, MG ZS EV, and the forthcoming Hyundai Creta EV.
pros: Unmatched service network and reliability from Maruti Suzuki
Specialized EV platform for enhanced cabin space
Top trim expected to offer segment-first AWD capability
Highly competitive starting price anticipated
cons: Late entry into a fiercely competitive mid-size EV market
Top-of-the-line AWD variant may exceed ₹22 Lakh

title: Kia Syros: The Boxy Compact SUV Ready to Disrupt the Indian Market
description: Perfectly positioned between the Sonet and Seltos, the new Kia Syros introduces a rugged, boxy exterior along with premium features in the compact SUV category.
content: Kia India is on track to solidify its presence in the SUV segment with the upcoming Syros, an innovative compact SUV poised for a mid-2026 launch.
In contrast to the sporty Sonet, the Syros embraces a tall, boxy, and robust design inspired by the flagship EV9, ensuring ample headroom and a commanding presence on the road.
Targeted as a premium family vehicle, the interior is set to feature dual 10.25-inch screens, a panoramic sunroof, ventilated seating, and Level 2 ADAS for enhanced safety.

Under the hood, the Syros is expected to offer powertrain options similar to the Sonet, including the dependable 1.2L naturally aspirated petrol and the dynamic 1.0L turbo-petrol, available with both manual and DCT transmissions.

With a price range anticipated between ₹12 Lakh and ₹17 Lakh, the Syros aims to deliver a mini-Seltos experience for those who value spacious interiors and cutting-edge technology over sporty performance.
pros: Unique boxy design provides exceptional cabin space and headroom
Well-equipped interior featuring a panoramic sunroof and Level 2 ADAS
Trusted and efficient engine options from the Kia lineup
cons: No diesel engine variant expected at launch
Boxy aerodynamics may slightly affect highway fuel efficiency

title: Volkswagen Taigun Facelift 2026 Launched: New Gearbox, ADAS & Price
description: The revamped 2026 Volkswagen Taigun facelift is officially launched! It showcases a new 8-speed automatic gearbox for the 1.0L TSI and Level 2 ADAS, gearing up to reclaim its place in the SUV segment.
content: Volkswagen India has unveiled the eagerly awaited mid-cycle facelift for the Taigun SUV as of April 9, 2026, featuring substantial enhancements in both mechanics and tech.

A major highlight is the newly introduced 8-speed torque converter automatic transmission for the 1.0L TSI engine, which replaces the older 6-speed model, promising improved highway performance and better fuel efficiency.
On the exterior, the Taigun boasts a more aggressive front design with an illuminated VW badge, redesigned matrix LED headlights, and a fresh set of 17-inch aero-style alloy wheels.
Inside, the cabin has received significant upgrades, including Level 2 ADAS, a 360-degree camera, and premium soft-touch materials on the dashboard, enhancing its upscale feel.
With prices beginning at ₹11.99 Lakh, the updated Taigun is well-positioned to compete with the Hyundai Creta, Kia Seltos, and the newly introduced Renault Duster.
pros: The new 8-speed automatic enhances the smoothness of the 1.0L TSI
Inclusion of Level 2 ADAS and a 360-degree camera
Exceptional driving dynamics and build quality
Illuminated logo and refreshed styling deliver impressive road presence
cons: 1.5L GT variants see a significant price increase
Rear seating remains more accommodating for two adults rather than three

title: 2026 Renault Duster Deliveries Kick Off This Week: The Icon Is Back!
description: The brand-new Renault Duster is set to grace Indian roads this April, starting at ₹10.49 Lakh. Here’s why it’s poised to be the ultimate competitor to the Creta with Level 2 ADAS.
content: The anticipation has come to an end! Following a grand re-launch in mid-March, the 2026 Renault Duster's deliveries are officially underway across India this week.
The third-generation Duster has already amassed over 50,000 bookings, with most customers choosing the powerful 1.3-litre turbo-petrol variants.
Renault has truly elevated the interior experience, outfitting the new Duster with a 10.1-inch touchscreen, Google built-in features, a panoramic sunroof, and ventilated front seats.
Safety takes center stage with 6 airbags as standard and the introduction of Level 2 ADAS, marking its debut in a Renault vehicle in India.
While deliveries of the turbo-petrol models are commencing now, those interested in the 1.8L Strong Hybrid variant will have to hold tight until Diwali 2026.
pros: Competitive starting price of ₹10.49 Lakh
Bold, rugged design that distinguishes it from ordinary crossovers
Superior ground clearance and suspension for challenging road conditions
Includes Level 2 ADAS and a 360-degree camera
cons: No diesel engine option is offered
Launch of the Strong Hybrid variant postponed until late 2026

title: Mahindra XUV.e8 (Electric XUV700): Expected Price, Range & 2026 Launch
description: Mahindra's flagship electric SUV, the XUV.e8, is set for a much-anticipated launch in mid-2026. Discover the battery specifications, all-wheel drive system, and cutting-edge interior design.
content: Mahindra is poised to lead the premium electric vehicle market with the XUV.e8, the all-electric iteration of the hugely popular XUV700.

Having been spotted testing across India, the XUV.e8 maintains the bold profile of its internal combustion engine (ICE) counterpart, featuring a sleek closed grille, a full-width LED light strip, and aerodynamic alloy wheels.

Inside, the cabin is a haven for technology enthusiasts, featuring an impressive triple-screen arrangement that spans the dashboard, incorporating AI-enhanced advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) and state-of-the-art connectivity tech.

Built on Mahindra's innovative INGLO skateboard platform, the vehicle is expected to come equipped with a substantial 80 kWh battery, available in both Rear-Wheel Drive (RWD) and high-performance All-Wheel Drive (AWD) variants.
With aggressive pricing aimed at undercutting luxury electric vehicles, the XUV.e8 is set to compete directly with the Tata Harrier EV and the BYD Atto 3 when it arrives in showrooms later this year.

specs.engine: Dual Motor AWD / Single Motor RWD

pros: Impressive 80 kWh battery for worry-free long-distance driving
Modern triple-screen dashboard design
Generous 3-row seating arrangement within an electric platform
Proven structural safety of the XUV700 coupled with INGLO EV technology
cons: Higher starting price expected compared to the ICE XUV700
Substantial kerb weight due to the large battery system

title: Toyota Urban Cruiser Ebella: Launch Date, Expected Price & Features
description: Toyota is gearing up to unveil the Urban Cruiser Ebella, its new electric SUV, in India on April 15, 2026. Discover details about its battery specifications, range, and pricing.
content: Toyota is making a significant entry into the Indian electric vehicle market with the much-awaited Urban Cruiser Ebella, set to debut on April 15, 2026.
Constructed on a specialized EV skateboard platform, the Ebella aims to blend Toyota's renowned reliability with cutting-edge electric mobility technology.
It’s anticipated to come equipped with a strong 60 kWh battery pack, offering a practical driving range of over 450 km on a single charge.
The interior will feature a modern cabin with a sizable touchscreen infotainment system, sophisticated connected car technology, and Level 2 ADAS for improved safety.
With an expected pricing range of ₹18 Lakh to ₹23 Lakh, the Ebella will compete head-to-head with the Maruti Suzuki eVitara, Tata Curvv EV, and MG ZS EV.
pros: Reputable reliability and build quality from Toyota
Roomy interior thanks to the dedicated EV skateboard platform
Standard Level 2 ADAS available in mid and top variants
cons: Higher anticipated starting price compared to ICE rivals
Development of public fast-charging infrastructure in Tier 2/3 cities is still in progress

title: Tata Sierra EV 2026: Bookings Open, Specs, and Range Details
description: The iconic Tata Sierra makes its comeback in an all-electric form. Discover the battery specifications, anticipated pricing, and interior highlights of the 2026 Sierra EV.
content: The moment we've all been waiting for has arrived! Tata Motors has officially launched bookings for the eagerly awaited Sierra EV, breathing new life into a legendary model for the electric age.
Remaining remarkably faithful to its heritage, the 2026 Sierra EV showcases the signature alpine windows, a spacious lounge-inspired interior, and a sleek, modern front design.
Built on Tata's cutting-edge Gen 2 Acti.ev platform, it guarantees ample cabin space, featuring a panoramic sunroof and standard Level 2 Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS).
The Sierra EV is projected to be available with two battery pack options, targeting a realistic range that should alleviate any concerns about running out of charge on long drives.
Situated just above the Harrier EV, the Sierra is set to tap into the premium lifestyle SUV market, going head-to-head with the forthcoming Mahindra XEV 9e.
pros: Iconic retro-modern design featuring alpine windows
Spacious, lounge-like seating in the rear
Advanced Level 2 ADAS and V2L (Vehicle to Load) technology
Impressive ground clearance suited for Indian roads
cons: Premium pricing may put it beyond the reach of budget-conscious buyers
Heavy kerb weight might impact driving dynamics

title: Hyundai Boulder Concept 2026: Features, Design & Is It a Ford Bronco Rival?
description: Explore the Hyundai Boulder Concept, a rugged off-road SUV with a ladder-frame structure, featuring impressive 37-inch tires, 'Art of Steel' design, and cutting-edge trail technology.
content: The Hyundai Boulder Concept makes a bold statement in the extreme off-road vehicle market, representing a significant departure from Hyundai's typical crossover style.
Constructed on a sturdy ladder-frame chassis, this 'Art of Steel' SUV boasts enormous 37-inch mud-terrain tires and remarkable ground clearance, ready for the toughest trails.
Inside, it harmonizes rugged practicality with high-tech features, including tactile physical knobs, fold-out tray tables, and an advanced real-time off-road navigation system.
The exterior is eye-catching with a striking Liquid Titanium finish, safari-style roof windows, coach doors, and a functional double-hinged tailgate.
Though it remains a concept for the time being, the Boulder platform hints at an eagerly awaited midsize pickup truck that is expected to begin production by 2030.
pros: Genuine ladder-frame off-road performance
Innovative double-hinged tailgate and coach doors
Striking, boxy design with impressive 37-inch tires
Sophisticated software-driven off-road guidance system
cons: Currently a concept vehicle only
Production model isn't expected until 2030
Mainly focused on the US market

title: Hyundai Verna 2026 Gets New Features Update
description: Hyundai Verna 2026 updated with new features, improved comfort, and better driving experience in the sedan segment.
content: With the new updates, the Hyundai Verna 2026 remains a strong contender in the sedan segment.
The latest version offers improved features, greater comfort, and improved driving dynamics.
It remains a top choice for customers seeking a premium sedan experience in India.
pros: Premium design
Comfortable ride quality
Feature-rich cabin
cons: Sedan demand lower than SUVs

title: Renault Duster 2026: Legendary SUV is Back in India
description: Renault Duster 2026: The legendary Renault Duster is back in India. This time, it has a bold new design, hybrid technology, and strong SUV credentials, making it one of the most trending cars to make a comeback in India.
content: Renault Duster 2026: Renault has launched a new version of one of India's most iconic SUVs.
The Renault Duster 2026 has a completely new exterior, modern features, and enhanced safety features.
The Renault Duster 2026 is a strong contender in the mid-size SUV segment.
pros: Strong brand comeback value
Modern SUV design with rugged DNA
Hybrid fuel efficiency options
Competitive pricing in segment
cons: Has to compete with the likes of Creta and Seltos
Top-end models can get pricey

title: Hyundai Creta 2026: Most Trending Family SUV
description: Hyundai Creta remains one of India's top-trending SUVs in 2026 thanks to premium features, comfort, and strong city-highway balance.
content: Creta continues to trend among buyers looking for a complete family SUV package.
It offers a feature-rich cabin, refined drive quality, and dependable day-to-day usability.
For city + highway mixed use, it stays one of the most searched options in India.
pros: Premium feature list
Comfortable ride
Strong resale
cons: Higher waiting in top variants

title: Kia Seltos 2026: Tech-Focused Trending SUV
description: Kia Seltos is trending for its design, connected features, and strong road presence in the midsize SUV segment.
content: Seltos attracts buyers who prioritize style and technology in daily driving.
Its cabin quality and infotainment setup remain key reasons for strong popularity.
For buyers wanting a modern SUV feel, Seltos continues to be a top shortlist car.
pros: Bold design
Loaded features
Great cabin feel
cons: Slightly firm low-speed ride

title: Tata Nexon 2026: Safety-Led Trending Compact SUV
description: Tata Nexon remains a trending compact SUV pick due to strong safety reputation, bold styling, and practical ownership value.
content: Nexon stays highly popular among safety-conscious buyers in India.
Its road presence, features, and balanced real-world usability keep demand strong.
For buyers comparing under-15-lakh SUVs, Nexon remains one of the most followed options.
pros: Strong safety image
Value for money
Good road stance
cons: Rear seat space could be better
